title: lazarum johnsonianum
intro: Lazarum johnsonianum, also known as Johnson's root, is a perennial plant native to North America, with a history of medicinal use by indigenous communities. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its root, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Lazarum johnsonianum include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Lazarum johnsonianum typically involve drying and boiling the root to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
